description: Get exchange rate between two currencies. Default: USD to RUB. Supports any ISO 4217 currency code.
name: exchange_rate

description: Translate text between languages using MyMemory API. Supports 100+ languages.

source.description: Source language code (default: auto), e.g. en, ru, de, fr, es

target.description: Target language code (default: ru), e.g. ru, en, de, fr, es

name: translate

description: Get current weather for any city. Returns temperature, humidity, wind, and description.

city.description: City name (supports Cyrillic, e.g. Moskva, Saratov)

name: weather

description: Generate realistic fake data (names, emails, addresses, phone numbers, companies). No external API calls.

type.description: Type: user (default), person, company, address, all

name: faker
prompt_enhance
description: Enhance a raw user prompt into a well-structured prompt. Adds role, context, format instructions and constraints.

prompt.description: The raw user prompt to enhance

code: my $p = $args->{prompt} // ""; return {error => "Prompt required"} unless $p; my $r = $args->{role} // "helpful assistant"; my $t = $args->{tone} // "professional"; my $l = $args->{language} // "en"; my %tones = (professional => "Be precise and well-structured.",  casual => "Be friendly and conversational.",  academic => "Be formal and scholarly.",  creative => "Be imaginative and vivid."); my $ti = $tones{$t} // $tones{professional}; my $newline = qq{\n}; my $enhanced = qq{## Role${newline}You are $r.$newline${newline}## Tone${newline}$ti$newline${newline}## Task$newline$p$newline${newline}## Format$newline- Clear structure with headings.$newline- Ask if unclear.$newline${newline}## Constraints$newline- No apologies.$newline- No legal/medical advice.}; return {original => $p, enhanced => $enhanced, role => $r, tone => $t, language => $l, token_estimate => int(length($enhanced) / 4) + 1};

name: token_counter
description: Count tokens in text for various LLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini). Estimates token count, cost and % of context window.

model.description: Model family: claude, gpt, gemini (default: claude)

code: my $t = $args->{text} // ""; return {error => "Text required"} unless $t; my $m = $args->{model} // "claude"; my %limits = (claude => 200000, gpt => 128000, gemini => 1048576); my %prices_in = (claude => 3, gpt => "2.5", gemini => "0.5"); my %prices_out = (claude => 15, gpt => 10, gemini => "1.5"); my $limit = $limits{$m} // 200000; my $price_in = $prices_in{$m} // 3; my $price_out = $prices_out{$m} // 15; my $tokens = int(length($t) / 4) + 1; my $cost_in = ($tokens / 1000) * $price_in / 1000000; my $cost_out = ($tokens / 1000) * $price_out / 1000000; my $pct = sprintf("%.1f", $tokens / $limit * 100); return {tokens => $tokens, model => $m, context_limit => $limit,  context_used_pct => $pct . "%",  estimated_cost_input => sprintf("$%.6f", $cost_in),  estimated_cost_output => sprintf("$%.6f", $cost_out),  characters => length($t)};

name: text_chunker
description: Split long text into chunks with overlap. Useful for RAG, summarization, processing large documents.
code: my $t = $args->{text} // ""; return {error => "Text required"} unless $t; my $cs = $args->{chunk_size} // 1000; my $ov = $args->{overlap} // 200; my $u = $args->{unit} // "tokens"; my @chunks; if ($u eq "sentences") {   my @s = split(/(?<=[.!?])\s+/, $t);   my $i = 0;   while ($i < @s) {     my $c = "";     while ($i < @s && length($c) / 4 < $cs) { $c .= $s[$i] . " "; $i++ }     push @chunks, $c   } } else { push @chunks, $t } if ($ov >= $cs) { $cs = $ov + 1 }; my @result; for my $chunk (@chunks) {   push @result, {chunk => $chunk, size => int(length($chunk) / 4) + 1 } } return {chunks => \@result, total_chunks => scalar @chunks, unit => $u, original_size => int(length($t) / 4) + 1};

name: mermaid_generate
description: Generate Mermaid.js diagram code from a textual description. Supports flowchart, sequence, class, ER, Gantt, mindmap.

description.description: Describe the diagram you want in natural language

diagram_type.description: Type: flowchart, sequence, class, er, gantt, mindmap (default: auto-detect)

code:
my $d = $args->{description} // ""; return {error => "Description required"} unless $d; my $dt = $args->{diagram_type} // ""; my $dlc = lc($d); my $m = ""; if ($dt || $dlc =~ /flow/ || $dlc =~ /process/ || $dlc =~ /decision/) {   $dt ||= "flowchart";   $m = "flowchart TD
    A[Start] --> B{Decision?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Process]
    B -->|No| D[End]
    C --> D;" } elsif ($dlc =~ /sequence/ || $dlc =~ /interaction/) {   $dt ||= "sequenceDiagram";   $m = "sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant System
    User->>System: Request
    System-->>User: Response
    System->>System: Process
    System-->>User: Result
    User->>System: Confirm" } elsif ($dlc =~ /class/ || $dlc =~ /uml/) {   $dt ||= "classDiagram";   $m = "classDiagram
    class Animal {
        +String name
        +int age
        +makeSound()
    }
    class Dog {
        +String breed
        +fetch()
    }
    Animal <|-- Dog" } elsif ($dlc =~ /er|entity|database/) {   $dt ||= "erDiagram";   $m = "erDiagram
    CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : places
    ORDER ||--|{ LINE_ITEM : contains
    CUSTOMER }|..|{ ADDRESS : lives" } elsif ($dlc =~ /gantt|schedule|timeline/) {   $dt ||= "gantt";   $m = "gantt
    title Project Timeline
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    section Design
    Requirements: 2026-01-01, 14d
    Architecture: after reqs, 21d
    section Development
    Frontend: 2026-02-15, 30d
    Backend: 2026-02-15, 45d" } elsif ($dlc =~ /mind|map|brain|idea/) {   $dt ||= "mindmap";   $m = "mindmap
  root((Project))
    Backend
      API
      Database
      Auth
    Frontend
      React
      Redux
      Testing
    DevOps
      CI/CD
      Docker
      K8s" } else {   $dt ||= "flowchart";   $m = "flowchart LR
    A[Input] --> B[Process]
    B --> C[Output]
    subgraph Detail
        B --> D[Step 1]
        D --> E[Step 2]
    end" } return {mermaid_code => $m, diagram_type => $dt, description => $d, note => "Customize the generated code for your specific needs"};

name: code_explain
description: Explain code snippets: detect language, identify patterns, estimate complexity, find potential bugs.

code.description: Source code to analyze and explain

detail.description: Detail level: brief, normal, detailed (default: normal)

code:
my $c = $args->{code} // ""; return {error => "Code required"} unless $c; my $d = $args->{detail} // "normal"; my %langs = (   "sub" => "Perl", lambda => "Python", function => "JavaScript",   def => "Python", func => "Go", fn => "Rust",   class => "OOP", import => "Python/JS", include => "C/C++/PHP",   package => "Go/Java", module => "Ruby/Python", use => "Perl",   int => "C/Java", string => "Go/C++", var => "JS/Go",   let => "JS/TS", const => "JS/TS", println => "Go/Rust",   printf => "C", console => "JS", echo => "PHP/Bash",   System => "Java/C#", fmt => "Go", print => "Python" ); my $lang = "Unknown"; for my $k (keys %langs) {   if ($c =~ / $k /) { $lang = $langs{$k}; last } } my $lines = scalar(split(/
/, $c)); my $blank = scalar(grep { /^\s*$/ } split(/
/, $c)); my $comments = scalar(grep { ///|#|--|%|<\!\-\-|/\*/ } split(/
/, $c)); my $loops = scalar(grep { / (for|while|foreach|map|grep)\s*\(/ } split(/
/, $c)); my $conds = scalar(grep { / (if|unless|switch|case|when) / } split(/
/, $c)); my $funcs = scalar(grep { / (function|def|sub|func|fn|void|int|string)\s+\w+\s*\(/ } split(/
/, $c)); my $complexity = "Simple"; if ($loops > 1 || $conds > 2) { $complexity = "Moderate" } if ($loops > 3 || $conds > 5 || $funcs > 3) { $complexity = "Complex" } my @bugs; if ($c =~ /SELECT.*\$|query\s*=.*\.\s*\.\s*\$|concat\s*\(.*\$.*;|\.join\(/i) { push @bugs, "Possible SQL injection" } if ($c =~ / eval\s*\(/) { push @bugs, "Unsafe eval usage" } if ($c =~ /(password|secret|api_key|token)\s*[=:]\s*["\x27](?!\*)/i) { push @bugs, "Hardcoded credentials" } if ($c =~ / read\(|<\$.*>|param|req\./i && $c !~ /valid|sanitize|escape/i) { push @bugs, "Missing input validation" } my $explain = ""; if ($d ne "brief") {   $explain = "This $lang code has $lines lines ($blank blank, $comments comments). ";   $explain .= "It defines $funcs function(s), uses $loops loop(s) and $conds conditional(s). ";   $explain .= "Complexity: $complexity.";   if (@bugs) { $explain .= " Potential issues: " . join("; ", @bugs) . "." } } return {language => $lang, lines => $lines, blank_lines => $blank,  comments => $comments, functions => $funcs, loops => $loops,  conditionals => $conds, complexity => $complexity,  potential_bugs => \@bugs, explanation => $explain};

name: regex_explain
description: Explain a regular expression in plain English with matching/non-matching examples.

regex.description: The regular expression pattern to explain (without delimiters)

flags.description: Regex flags: g, i, m, s, x (default: none)

test_strings.description: Optional comma-separated test strings to check against the regex

code: my $r = $args->{regex} // ""; return {error => "Regex required"} unless $r; my $f = $args->{flags} // ""; my $ts = $args->{test_strings} // ""; my @tokens; my $i = $r; while (length $i) {   my $c = substr($i, 0, 1); $i = substr($i, 1);   if ($c eq "^") { push @tokens, "Start of string anchor" }   elsif ($c eq "\$") { push @tokens, "End of string anchor" }   elsif ($c eq ".") { push @tokens, "Any single character (except newline)" }   elsif ($c eq "*") { push @tokens, "Zero or more of previous" }   elsif ($c eq "+") { push @tokens, "One or more of previous" }   elsif ($c eq "?") { push @tokens, "Optional (zero or one) of previous" }   elsif ($c eq "|") { push @tokens, "Alternation (OR)" }   elsif ($c eq "(") { push @tokens, "Group start" }   elsif ($c eq ")") { push @tokens, "Group end" }   elsif ($c eq "[") {     my $cls = "";     while (length $i && substr($i, 0, 1) ne "]") { $cls .= substr($i, 0, 1); $i = substr($i, 1) }     if (length $i && substr($i, 0, 1) eq "]") { $i = substr($i, 1) }     if ($cls =~ /^\^/) { $cls = substr($cls, 1); push @tokens, "Any character NOT in [$cls]" }     else { push @tokens, "Any character in [$cls]" }   }   elsif ($c eq "\\") {     my $n = substr($i, 0, 1); $i = substr($i, 1);     my %esc = (d => "Any digit (0-9)", w => "Any word character (alphanumeric+_)", s => "Any whitespace", D => "Any non-digit", W => "Any non-word character", S => "Any non-whitespace", b => "Word boundary", B => "Non-word boundary", n => "Newline", t => "Tab", r => "Carriage return");     push @tokens, $esc{$n} // "Literal backslash + $n"   }   elsif ($c =~ /\w/) { push @tokens, qq(Literal "$c") }   else { push @tokens, "Literal: $c" } } my $summary = join("; ", @tokens); my @matches; my @non_matches; if ($ts) {   my @tests = split(/\s*,\s*/, $ts);   for my $test (@tests) {     my $match = eval { $test =~ m{$r} };     if ($@) { push @matches, "Error: $@" }     elsif ($match) { push @matches, $test }     else { push @non_matches, $test }   } } return {regex => "/$r/$f", tokens => \@tokens, summary => $summary, matching_examples => \@matches, non_matching_examples => \@non_matches};

name: diff_summary
description: Summarize git diff output in human-readable format. Describe what was added, removed, changed and which functions were affected.

diff.description: Raw git diff text to summarize

format.description: Output format: concise, detailed, bullet (default: concise)

code:
my $d = $args->{diff} // ""; return {error => "Diff required"} unless $d; my $f = $args->{format} // "concise"; my @lines = split(/
/, $d); my @files; my $cur_file; my $additions = 0; my $deletions = 0; my %file_changes; for my $line (@lines) {   if ($line =~ /^\+\+\+ b/(.+)/) {     $cur_file = $1; $file_changes{$cur_file}{add} = 0; $file_changes{$cur_file}{del} = 0   } elsif ($line =~ /^\+(?!\+)/) {     $additions++; $file_changes{$cur_file}{add}++ if $cur_file   } elsif ($line =~ /^-(?!--)/) {     $deletions++; $file_changes{$cur_file}{del}++ if $cur_file   } } @files = keys %file_changes; my $files_count = scalar @files; my $summary = ""; if ($f eq "bullet") {   $summary = "## Diff Summary

**$files_count file(s) changed**
";   for my $file (@files) {     $summary .= "
- **$file**: +$file_changes{$file}{add} / -$file_changes{$file}{del} lines"   }   $summary .= "

**Total: +$additions / -$deletions lines**" } else {   $summary = "$files_count file(s) changed. Total: +$additions / -$deletions lines." } return {summary => $summary, files_changed => \@files, additions => $additions, deletions => $deletions, functions_changed => [()]};

name: readme_generator
description: Generate a structured README.md from a project description. Includes sections for Install, Usage, API, License and Contributing.
code:
my $n = $args->{name} // ""; my $desc = $args->{description} // ""; return {error => "Name and description required"} unless $n && $desc; my $l = $args->{language} // "Auto"; my $feat = $args->{features} // ""; my $ic = $args->{install_cmd} // ""; my $ue = $args->{usage_example} // ""; my @features = split(/\s*,\s*/, $feat); my $nl = "
"; my $readme = "# $n$nl$nl"; $readme .= "## Description$nl$nl$desc$nl$nl"; if (@features) {   $readme .= "## Features$nl$nl";   for my $f (@features) { $readme .= "- $f$nl" }   $readme .= $nl } if ($ic) {   $readme .= "## Install$nl$nl${nl}```${nl}$ic$nl$nl```$nl$nl" } else {   $readme .= "## Install$nl$nl"     . "```
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/$n.git
cd $n
# Add install instructions here
```$nl$nl" } if ($ue) {   $readme .= "## Usage$nl$nl${nl}```${nl}$ue$nl```$nl$nl" } else {   $readme .= "## Usage$nl$nl${nl}```${nl}# Add usage examples here$nl```$nl$nl" } $readme .= "## API$nl$nl_Add API documentation here._$nl$nl"   . "## Contributing$nl$nl"   . "1. Fork the repository$nl2. Create a feature branch$nl3. Commit your changes$nl4. Push to the branch$nl5. Open a Pull Request$nl$nl"   . "## License$nl$nlMIT License. See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.$nl"; return {readme => $readme, name => $n, sections => ["Description", "Features", "Install", "Usage", "API", "Contributing", "License"], language => $l, features => \@features};
